reasoning: The document has 9 major defects across LLM fidelity and programmatic signals, which exceeds the threshold of 5 for a HUMAN_REVIEW routing. Issues include extensive visual duplication of text as full-page images, multiple list hierarchy and formatting errors, retained header/footer elements, displaced hyperlinks, and degraded fallback image rendering with incomplete alt text. Based on the count-based routing rules, this triggers an automatic REJECT.

concerns.analysis: There are list hierarchy flattening and missing numbering issues. On pages 11-12 of the source document, under section 'B. Information to be Provided in Blasting Plans', there is a sub-list starting with '1. The information provided...' which itself contains indented sub-items ('a.', 'b.', 'c.', etc.). In the output, items 'a.' through 'j.' are rendered as siblings to '1.' rather than being nested underneath it. Additionally, list item '2. If the maximum explosive weight...' from page 12 of the
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
1. The information provided by Alamance Aggregates in each Blasting Plan submitted to Colonial and NCDEQ shall include all of the following:
a. Date and time of the proposed blast.

concerns.analysis: The HTML output contains multiple instances where large image crops of the PDF pages are embedded alongside the text that has already been extracted, causing the user to see the same content twice. For instance, on page 7, the text for numbered paragraphs 4 and 5 is extracted as HTML lists, but an image of that exact text is also embedded immediately after. A similar duplication occurs with the signature block and cc list on page 10.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 7 shows numbered paragraphs 4 and 5 ("Web-based Data Room..." and "Permit Conditions...").
concerns.output_quote: The HTML contains the extracted text for these paragraphs, followed immediately by an image displaying the same content ().
concerns.location: Page 7 content (after paragraph 5) and Page 10 signature block

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (8 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=8. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 16 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=16. Examples: p1: "Official letterhead for North Carolina Environment"; p1: "The bottom portion of a formal letter showing para"; p2: "The concluding portion of an official letter regar". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 28 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=28, examples=['mailto:report.spam@nc.gov', 'http://www.colpipe.com', 'http://www.colpipe.com']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (17): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio; 1; 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=17, pages_affected=16/17. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (1 instance(s)): p6→p7: list item ends “…rgency Access field roadway. To ensure a”, continues as paragraph
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=1. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly: 1 ordered list(s) have alphabetical markers (A, B, C) rendered as numeric — e.g. "a. In the event that any of the information provided to Colo"
concerns.analysis: alpha_numeric_confusion=1. Ordered list items start with sequential letter prefixes (A., B., C.) but are rendered as numeric lists, losing the alphabetical semantics.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 12 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (12 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "... featuring the state seal and listing Governor Roy"; p1: "...ormation to complete an application process. Below"; p2: "...ermit application deadline. It features a sign-off"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=12 (truncated=12, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

Identified two instances of visual emphasis and structural loss in Attachment 1. First, multiple headings and list introductions are underlined in the source PDF (e.g., 'I. BLASTING REQUIREMENTS AND TECHNICAL CONDITIONS' and 'A. Notice of Blasting Related Activities.'), but this underline emphasis is completely lost in the output. Second, the list marker '2.' was dropped from the paragraph regarding maximum explosive weight variations.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
1) Underlined text: "I. BLASTING REQUIREMENTS AND TECHNICAL CONDITIONS"
2) List marker: "2. If the maximum explosive weight per delay will vary during the shot..."
concerns.output_quote:
1) No underline markup: "I. BLASTING REQUIREMENTS AND TECHNICAL CONDITIONS"
2) Missing marker: "If the maximum explosive weight per delay will vary during the shot..."

action_items: Remove duplicated page images that represent already extracted text.
Correct list hierarchies and formatting, particularly around alphabetical vs numeric markers and flattened sub-items.
Strip header and footer artifacts from the body content.
Ensure hyperlinks are inline rather than rendered as standalone blocks.
Provide complete and descriptive alt text for all meaningful images.
